home.overview: FiCR is a fire-compartmentation-and-risk ontology for AEC/FM digital-twin records. It models building elements, compartment and risk-unit boundaries, fire-resistance evidence, boundary assumptions, regulatory requirements, compliance findings, and inspection workflow records for researchers and practitioners who need traceable fire-safety assessment data.

sampleReport.title: Duplex A memo sample report
sampleReport.source: Generated from the memo-derived ABox, CQ results, and gap check.

A: A1 Building Overview: 1 result; building BLD-DA, 3 storeys, 13 spaces.
A2 Storey Inventory: 3 results; storeys listed: Ground Floor, Level 2, Flat Roof.
A3 Space Ledger: 13 results; space ledger includes named room and roof spaces.
A4 Space Usage Distribution: 5 results; usage categories: HabitableRoom 5, Bathroom 2, EntranceHall 1, Kitchen 1, ServiceUsage 1.
A5 Element Inventory: 4 results; element inventory: Wall 2, Alarm 1, Doorset 1, RoofSlab 1.
A6 Per Space Fire Protection: 1 result; fire-protection item recorded in A105 Entrance Hall.
B: B1 Compliance Health Score: 0 results; no compliance health-score rows because no compliance assessment records are present.
B2 Element Rei Detail: 0 results; no REI requirement comparison rows because no regulatory requirement records are present.
B3 Owl Inferred Classification: 1 result; inferred class summary reports Wall 2.
B4 Finding Traceability: 0 results; no finding traceability rows because no compliance findings are present.
C: C1 Risk Unit Inventory: 2 results; risk units: Unit A covers 10 spaces; Unit B covers 2 spaces.
C2 Boundary Condition Distribution: 4 results; boundary states by unit: riskunit_A: Compromised 1, riskunit_A: Unknown 3, riskunit_B: Compromised 1, riskunit_B: Unknown 3.
C3 Evidence Completeness: 8 results; 6 evidence gaps and 2 supported assumptions.
C4 Risk Unit Confidence: 2 results; risk priority rows: riskunit_A score 7, riskunit_B score 7.
C5 Gap Consistency: 0 results; no contradictory gap-consistency rows.
C6 Risk Finding Traceability: 0 results; no risk-finding traceability rows because findings are absent.
D: D1 Inspection Workflow: 1 result; workflow task: Arrange firestopping contractor follow-up next week.
D2 Assessment Results: 0 results; no assessment-result rows because assessment records are absent.

actions: Unsealed pipe penetration through the party wall on Level 2 roughly above the kitchen area is linked to compromised compartmentation for riskunit_A and riskunit_B; treat the party-wall penetration as a firestopping action item before relying on the compartment boundary.
6 boundary assumptions returned an evidence gap; supplement the record with inspection evidence for unknown cavity-barrier, external-spread, and structural-stability assumptions.
D2 is unanswerable because ComplianceChecking and producesFinding are missing; add assessment records linked to compliance outputs before reporting assessment results.
B2 and B4 return no rows; add regulatory requirement and compliance finding records before presenting REI comparison or finding traceability as an ontology-backed judgment.

advisoryNotes.riskMechanism: The demo records a compromised compartmentation assumption for both risk units, supported by the observed unsealed pipe penetration through the party wall. If the service opening is not sealed to the wall resistance, fire and smoke can bypass the separating construction.
advisoryNotes.remedialMeasure: Inspect the penetration, identify the pipe/service type and aperture size, and install a tested proprietary fire-stopping system or an ADB-compatible pipe penetration treatment that maintains the fire resistance of the separating element. Capture before/after photographs and product/test evidence.
advisoryNotes.priority: Priority 1: arrange firestopping verification and remedial works before relying on the Unit A / Unit B boundary as intact compartmentation.

advisoryNotes.riskMechanism: The CQ output carries evidence gaps for unknown cavity-barrier, external-spread, and structural-stability assumptions. The memo also states that roof access was not obtained, so the roof/external-spread evidence remains incomplete rather than verified.
advisoryNotes.remedialMeasure: Schedule a follow-up inspection with roof access and targeted checks to collect photographs, notes, and document references for the unknown assumptions. Keep each evidence item linked to the specific boundary assumption it supports.
advisoryNotes.priority: Priority 2: resolve missing evidence after the party-wall penetration is controlled, then update Unknown assumptions only where the new evidence supports a clear state.

advisoryNotes.riskMechanism: The demo has no regulatory requirement records, no compliance assessments, and no produced findings, so REI comparison and assessment-result questions cannot produce ontology-backed compliance conclusions.
advisoryNotes.remedialMeasure: Select the applicable regulatory basis, add requirement records for the relevant separating elements, and record compliance assessments that produce findings. Where using England guidance, reference the current GOV.UK Approved Document B route and any accepted project-specific fire-engineered alternative.
advisoryNotes.priority: Priority 3: complete the regulatory and assessment layer after field evidence is updated, so later reports can separate evidence gaps from formal compliance findings.

current: static site without live LLM or chatbot
nearTerm: richer input-format handling for existing text and TTL lanes
saved query sets and repeatable endpoint checks
vision: multimodal ingestion for point cloud, RGBD, and drawings via the same JSON/TTL contracts
cross-source entity resolution before merged assessment workflows
